title: Novel steroid hormone ligand assays
abstract: The present invention provides diagnostic test kits and assay methods involving the detection of steroid hormone ligand(s) in a test sample. For example, in the detection of ligands which bind to and activate an androgen receptor, estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor, mineralocorticoid receptor and glucocorticoid receptor. In particular, the test kits and assay methods described herein are configured for field-based applications, for example, for trackside drug testing in athletes or at home testing of humans or animals for steroid hormone activity, which test kits, assays and methods colorimetric read-outs involving either inorganic pyrophosphate or CRISPR/Cas mediated RNA probe cleavage involving (e.g.) probes which have a detectable label including fluorophore/quencher molecules or fluorophore/tag molecules. Accordingly, the diagnostic test kits and assay methods described herein eliminate the requirement for detection instrumentation allowing reproducible 'rapid fire' tests in the field.
claims:
Claims - 78 -CLAIMS 1. A test kit for screening a sample for the presence of a ligand capable of eliciting a steroid hormone genomic response, the test kit comprising: (i) a steroid hormone receptor that is capable of forming a ligand-receptor complex with a ligand from the sample; (ii) a first nucleic acid molecule comprising: (a) a single polypeptide RNA polymerase promoter sequence; (b) a response element that is capable of being bound by the ligandreceptor complex; and (c) a CRISPR Cas RNA (crRNA) recognition sequence; wherein the response element (b) is located between the promoter sequence (a) and the crRNA recognition sequence (c), and wherein the first nucleic acid molecule optionally comprises a spacer sequence (d) located between the polymerase promoter sequence (a) and the response element (b), provided that [(a), (b) and (c)] or [(a), (d), (b) and (c)] are operably linked; (iii) a second nucleic acid molecule comprising a crRNA sequence which is complimentary to the crRNA recognition sequence in the first nucleic acid molecule; (iv) a CRISPR-activated RNA-targeting endonuclease; (v) an RNA probe comprising at least one detectable label; and (vi) optionally, a RNA polymerase. 2. The test kit according to claim 1, wherein the RNA polymerase promoter sequence is a T7 polymerase promoter sequence defined by SEQ ID NO: 34, and the RNA polymerase is T7 RNA polymerase. 3. The test kit according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the CRISPR-activated RNA- targeting endonucleaseis Cas 13, including Casl3a. 4. The test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the RNA probe comprises a fluorophore-RNA-quencher or FAM-RNA-biotin. 5. The test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the test kit is configured to measure: 

 - 79 - (i) a ligand which binds and activates an androgen receptor, and the first nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists in a sequence defined by SEQ ID NO: 1; (ii) a ligand which binds and activates an estrogen receptor, and the first nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists in a sequence defined by SEQ ID NO: 2; (iii) a ligand which binds and activates a progesterone receptor, and the first nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists in a sequence defined by SEQ ID NO: 3; (iv) a ligand which binds and activates a mineralocorticoid receptor, and the first nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists in a sequence defined by SEQ ID NO: 4; or (v) a ligand which binds and activates a glucocorticoid receptor, and the first nucleic acid molecule comprises or consists in a sequence defined by SEQ ID NO: 5. 6. The test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the second nucleic acid sequence is defined by SEQ ID NO: 17. 7. The test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 6, further comprising ribonucleoside triphosphates. 8. The test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 7, further comprising at least one steroid hormone receptor coactivator and/or at least one steroid hormone receptor co repressor. 9. The test kit according to claim 8 wherein the steroid hormone receptor corepressor is selected from at least one of: (A) heat shock protein 90 (HSP90); (B) a complex of HSP90 and heat shock protein 70 (HSP70); (C) a complex of HSP90, HSP70 and heat shock protein 40 (HSP40); (D) a complex of HSP90, HSP70, HSP40 and p23; (E) a complex of HSP90, HSP70, HSP40, p23 and heat shock protein organizing protein (Hop); (F) a complex of HSP90, HSP70, HSP40, p23, Hop and 48kD Hip protein (Hip); (G) a complex of HSP90, HSP70, HSP40, p23, Hop, Hip and p60; (H) a complex of HSP90, HSP70, HSP40, p23, Hop, Hip, p60 and FKBP52; 

 - 80 - (I) erythroblast transformation-specific transcription factor ERG; and (J) at least one steroid receptor coactivator selected from SRC-1, SRC-2, SRC-3; and (K) any combination of (A) to (J). 10. The test kit according to claim 8 or claim 9, wherein the at least one steroid hormone receptor coactivator is selected from the group consisting of erythroblast transformation-specific transcription factor ERG; pl60 coactivators inclusive of steroid receptor coactivators, SRC-1, SRC-2, SRC-3; Vav3 a Rho GTPase guanine nucleotide exchange factor; E2F1; ATAD2; CBP/p300; Leupaxin; FHL2; the ARA family of proteins; GRIP1; BRAC1; and Zacl. 11. An assay method for detecting a ligand in a sample which ligand is capable of eliciting a steroid hormone genomic response, the assay method comprising the steps of: (1) contacting a sample with: (i) a steroid hormone receptor that forms a ligand-receptor complex with a ligand from the sample; (ii) a first nucleic acid molecule comprising: (a) a RNA polymerase promoter sequence; (b) a response element that is capable of being bound by the ligand-receptor complex; and (c) a CRISPR Cas RNA (crRNA) recognition sequence; wherein the response element (b) is located between the promoter sequence (a) and the crRNA recognition sequence (c), and wherein the first nucleic acid molecule optionally comprises spacer sequence (d) located between the polymerase promoter sequence (a) and the response element (b), provided that [(a), (b) and (c)] or [(a), (d), (b) and (c)] are operably linked; (iii) a second nucleic acid molecule comprising a crRNA sequence which is complimentary to the crRNA recognition sequence in the first nucleic acid molecule; (iv) a CRISPR-activated RNA-targeting endonuclease; (v) an RNA probe comprising at least one detectable label; (vi) a RNA polymerase; and (vii) ribonucleoside triphosphates, 

 - 81 - (2) measuring a change in the amount of detectable label associated with the RNA probe caused by binding of the ligand-receptor complex to the response element, wherein, a measured change in the amount of detectable label associated with the RNA probe reflects detection of a ligand in the sample. 12. A method according to claim 12, further comprising contacting the sample with at least one steroid hormone receptor coactivator and/or at least one steroid hormone receptor corepressor. 13. A method for determining the doping status of an athlete, the method comprising mixing a biological sample obtained from the athlete with the components of the test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 10 to ascertain if there is a change in the amount of detectable label associated with the RNA probe, wherein a change in the amount of detectable label associated with the RNA probe provides information about the doping status of the athlete. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein the athlete is selected from a human athlete, an equine athlete, a canine athlete and a camelid athlete. 15. A method for determining the steroid hormone receptor status of a human or animal, the method comprising mixing a biological sample obtained from the human or animal with the components of the test kit according to any one of claims 1 to 10 to ascertain if there is a change in the amount of detectable label associated with the RNA probe, wherein a change in the amount of detectable label associated with the RNA probe is measured using a lateral flow or other portable device.
